description: Catena shared supply-chain / dependency policy. Extend it from any repo with: extends: github>catenahq/scanctl:supply-chain

Adoption cooldown: minimumReleaseAge holds a release for 7 days. internalChecksFilter=strict means Renovate does not create the branch or PR until the release has aged past the cooldown, so CI (scanctl) scans the exact version that will merge. Without this, Renovate would open the PR on release day (scanners run on the day-0 version) and then auto-merge 7 days later with no fresh scan, defeating the cooldown. prCreation is left at its default (immediate): strict already holds opening until day 7, and not-pending would only stall PRs in repos whose CI runs on the PR rather than on branch push.
Pins and digest pins are exempted from the cooldown below: they carry no release age, so timestamp-required + strict would otherwise mark them pending forever and never open the pin PR.
Security fixes are exempt: vulnerabilityAlerts sets minimumReleaseAge=null and automerges, so a known-CVE patch is not held for 7 days.

No top-level schedule: timing is controlled solely by the single self-hosted Renovate cron (catenahq/renovate, daily). A Renovate-internal schedule (e.g. before 5am) silently no-ops every run that lands outside the window -- a delayed scheduled run OR any manual workflow_dispatch -- so the same invocation behaves differently by wall clock. Dropping it makes every run, scheduled or manual, do identical work. The 7-day minimumReleaseAge cooldown still throttles churn; lockFileMaintenance keeps its own weekly cadence below.

packageRules.description: Pins and digest pins carry no release age; never hold them in the adoption cooldown (timestamp-required + strict would otherwise keep them pending forever and never open the pin PR).
Low-risk GitHub Actions version bumps: group + automerge after the cooldown.

Do not chase github-actions digest (same-tag SHA) updates. A SHA has no release timestamp, so minimumReleaseAge cannot age it (timestamp-required marks it pending forever), and auto-adopting a moved tag re-introduces the mutable-tag supply-chain risk that SHA-pinning exists to prevent (cf. tj-actions/changed-files, CVE-2025-30066). New action code is adopted only via aged version bumps, which DO carry a release timestamp and pass through the 7-day cooldown.
Container image / compose bumps: group, review manually.
